Data Structures

struct  list_t
 Generic dynamic array of fixed-size items. More...
 

Functions

static list_t * list_new (size_t initial_capacity, size_t item_size)
 Allocate a new list.
 
static void list_clear (list_t *list, void(*free_func)(void *))
 Remove all items from the list, optionally freeing each one.
 
static void list_free (list_t *list, void(*free_func)(void *))
 Free the list and all of its items.
 
static bool list_add (list_t *list, void *item)
 Append an item to the list, growing it if necessary.
 
static void * list_get (const list_t *list, size_t index)
 Access an item by index.
 
static bool list_set (const list_t *list, size_t index, void *item)
 Overwrite an item at the given index.
 
static bool list_remove (list_t *list, size_t index, void(*free_func)(void *))
 Remove an item at the given index, shifting subsequent items.
 
static size_t list_size (const list_t *list)
 Return the current number of items.
 
static size_t list_capacity (const list_t *list)
 Return the allocated capacity.
 
static int list_check_valid (const list_t *list, size_t expected_item_size, bool must_be_non_empty)
 Validate a list_t instance for basic integrity.
 

Detailed Description

Author
IMD Software Team
Attention

Copyright (c) 2026 STMicroelectronics. All rights reserved.

This software is licensed under terms that can be found in the LICENSE file in the root directory of this software component. If no LICENSE file comes with this software, it is provided AS-IS.

Provides the list_t dynamic array type together with creation, manipulation, iteration and validation helpers. Several higher-level collection types (streams_t, controls_t, properties_t, etc.) are defined as type aliases of list_t.

Function Documentation

◆ list_add()

static bool list_add ( list_t * list,
void * item )
inlinestatic
Parameters
[in,out]listPointer to the list.
[in]itemPointer to the item to copy into the list.
Returns
true on success, false if reallocation failed.

◆ list_capacity()

static size_t list_capacity ( const list_t * list)
inlinestatic
Parameters
[in]listPointer to the list.
Returns
Maximum number of items before the next reallocation.

◆ list_check_valid()

static int list_check_valid ( const list_t * list,
size_t expected_item_size,
bool must_be_non_empty )
inlinestatic

This function checks that the list pointer is not NULL, the items pointer is not NULL, the item size matches the expected type, the capacity is not zero, and the size does not exceed the capacity. If must_be_non_empty is true, the list must also not be empty.

Parameters
listPointer to the list_t instance.
expected_item_sizeThe expected size of each item (e.g., sizeof(stream_t)).
must_be_non_emptyIf true, the list must not be empty; if false, empty lists are allowed.
Returns
MEDIA_ERROR_NONE if valid, or an appropriate MEDIA_ERROR_* code.

void(* free_func )(void *) )

[in,out]listPointer to the list.
[in]free_funcPer-item destructor (may be NULL).

size_t index )

Pointer to the item storage, or NULL if index is out of range.

static list_t * list_new ( size_t initial_capacity,
size_t item_size )
inlinestatic
Parameters
[in]initial_capacityNumber of items to pre-allocate.
[in]item_sizeSize in bytes of a single item.
Returns
Pointer to the newly created list, or NULL on allocation failure.

size_t index,

Parameters

true on success, false if index is out of range.
